notes: Row count updated from spec estimate (3,000-5,000) to actual (6,097 Bronze, 5,903 Silver) per EDA. has_scorecard_match expectation corrected from spec's 60-90% to 0% — the CIP granularity mismatch (6-digit crosswalk vs 4-digit Scorecard) means zero direct matches under strict matching. Spec acknowledges this and defers resolution to Gold zone.

rules.name: CIP code format: XX.XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE cipcode IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(cipcode, '^\d{2}\.\d{4}$')
rules.description: Every cipcode must match the CIP 2020 standard format XX.XXXX (2-digit family, dot, 4-digit detail). CIP codes must never be parsed as floats. Any format violation indicates an ingestor parsing failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: All 6,097 rows match ^\d{2}\.\d{4}$ with zero violations. 100% format compliance confirmed. Domain context requires CIPCODE always be treated as string type.

rules.name: SOC code format: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE soc_code IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: Every soc_code must match the SOC 2018 standard format XX-XXXX (2-digit major group, hyphen, 4-digit detail). Includes the 99-9999 no-match sentinel which is valid at Bronze.
rules.rationale: EDA: All 6,097 rows match ^\d{2}-\d{4}$ with zero violations. 100% format compliance confirmed including 194 sentinel rows (99-9999).

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: cipcode x soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT cipcode, soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.cip_soc_crosswalk GROUP BY cipcode, soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per CIP-SOC pairing (cipcode x soc_code). Any duplicate pair indicates an ingestor parsing or promote failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: 6,097 total rows with zero duplicates on the (cipcode, soc_code) composite key. Grain is perfectly unique in the source XLSX.

rules.name: Row count in expected range: 5,500-6,500
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) BETWEEN 5500 AND 6500 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM raw.cip_soc_crosswalk
rules.description: Bronze table must contain between 5,500 and 6,500 rows. This range is centered on the actual 6,097 with margin for minor NCES updates.
rules.rationale: EDA: Actual row count is 6,097 (above spec estimate of 3,000-5,000). Range set to 5,500-6,500 to allow for minor crosswalk revisions without false alarms while catching major deviations.

rules.name: No nulls on any source field
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE cipcode IS NULL OR cip_title IS NULL OR soc_code IS NULL OR soc_title IS NULL
rules.description: All four source fields (cipcode, cip_title, soc_code, soc_title) must be non-null. The crosswalk source has no missing values.
rules.rationale: EDA: Zero nulls across all 4 fields in all 6,097 rows (0% null rate on every field). Source XLSX is completely populated.

rules.name: No nulls on metadata fields
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE ingested_at IS NULL OR source_url IS NULL OR source_method IS NULL OR load_date IS NULL
rules.description: All metadata fields (ingested_at, source_url, source_method, load_date) must be non-null. These are generated by the pipeline and should never be missing.
rules.rationale: Pipeline metadata fields are generated at ingestion time. Null values indicate an ingestor bug. Physical model declares all fields NOT NULL.

rules.name: source_method is 'xlsx_download'
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE source_method != 'xlsx_download'
rules.description: The source_method field must always be 'xlsx_download' for this ingestor. Any other value indicates a pipeline configuration error.
rules.rationale: Physical model DDL includes CHECK (source_method = 'xlsx_download'). This is a constant value set by the ingestor.

rules.name: No-match sentinel count: ~194 rows with SOC 99-9999
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) BETWEEN 180 AND 210 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM raw.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE soc_code = '99-9999'
rules.description: The Bronze table should contain approximately 194 no-match sentinel rows (soc_code = '99-9999'). These represent CIP programs with no SOC correspondence. Count tracked for monitoring.
rules.rationale: EDA: Exactly 194 rows have soc_code = '99-9999' (3.18% of total). Range 180-210 allows for minor crosswalk revisions. P2 because this is informational -- the Silver zone handles filtering.

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: record_id
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The surrogate key record_id must be unique. It is a deterministic hash of (cipcode, soc_code) with prefix 'xw'. Any duplicate indicates a grain computation or promote failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: Zero duplicates on the (cipcode, soc_code) composite key in 6,097 source rows. Since record_id is a deterministic hash of this key, uniqueness is guaranteed if the source grain is clean.

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: cipcode x soc_code (natural key)
rules.sql: SELECT cipcode, soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk GROUP BY cipcode, soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The composite natural key (cipcode, soc_code) must have zero duplicates. This is the fundamental grain of the crosswalk.
rules.rationale: EDA: Zero duplicates confirmed on (cipcode, soc_code) across 6,097 source rows. After filtering 194 no-match rows, 5,903 valid pairs remain with perfect uniqueness.

rules.name: No-match rows excluded: zero rows with SOC 99-9999
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE soc_code = '99-9999'
rules.description: The Silver table must contain zero rows with the no-match sentinel soc_code '99-9999'. These are filtered out during transformation. Any presence indicates a filter failure.
rules.rationale: EDA: 194 no-match rows in Bronze with soc_code = '99-9999'. Spec requires these be excluded in Silver. After filtering, 5,903 valid rows remain. Physical model documents this as a filtering rule.

rules.name: CIP code format: XX.XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE cipcode IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(cipcode, '^\d{2}\.\d{4}$')
rules.description: Every cipcode in Silver must match XX.XXXX format. CIP codes must remain as strings, never converted to floats.
rules.rationale: EDA: 100% format compliance across all 6,097 source rows. Logical model validation rule requires regex ^\d{2}\.\d{4}$ with row rejection on failure.

rules.name: SOC code format: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE soc_code IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: Every soc_code in Silver must match XX-XXXX format. No sentinel codes should be present (those are filtered).
rules.rationale: EDA: 100% format compliance in source. After excluding 99-9999 sentinels, all remaining 5,903 rows have valid XX-XXXX SOC codes.

rules.name: SOC major group: valid 22 codes
rules.description: The soc_major_group field (first 2 chars of soc_code) must be one of the 23 valid SOC major group codes (22 civilian + 55 Military). After filtering 99-9999 sentinels, no group 99 should remain.
rules.rationale: EDA: 23 SOC major groups observed including group 99 (sentinel). After filtering sentinels, all remaining rows have valid major groups from the standard 22. Physical model CHECK constraint enforces this.

rules.name: match_quality: valid enum values only
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE match_quality NOT IN ('full', 'partial_no_onet', 'partial_no_bls', 'scorecard_only', 'no_scorecard')
rules.description: match_quality must be one of the 5 defined values. Any other value indicates a derivation logic bug in the CASE expression.
rules.rationale: Logical model defines an exhaustive 5-tier CASE expression with no ELSE/NULL branch. Physical model CHECK constraint enforces the valid set. EDA confirms the distribution maps to these 5 values.

rules.name: Row count in expected range: 5,500-6,200
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) BETWEEN 5500 AND 6200 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk
rules.description: Silver table must contain between 5,500 and 6,200 rows. This is the Bronze count (6,097) minus the no-match sentinel rows (194), yielding 5,903 expected.
rules.rationale: EDA: 6,097 Bronze rows minus 194 no-match sentinels = 5,903 valid Silver rows. Range 5,500-6,200 allows for minor crosswalk revisions while catching filter or transformation failures.

rules.name: No nulls on any Silver field
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE record_id IS NULL OR cipcode IS NULL OR cip_title IS NULL OR cip_family IS NULL OR soc_code IS NULL OR soc_title IS NULL OR soc_major_group IS NULL OR has_scorecard_match IS NULL OR has_bls_match IS NULL OR has_onet_match IS NULL OR match_quality IS NULL OR source_load_date IS NULL OR ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: All 13 Silver columns must be non-null. The logical model declares all fields NOT NULL with no exceptions. Invalid rows are rejected, not stored with nulls.
rules.rationale: EDA: Zero nulls across all 4 source fields in 6,097 rows. All derived fields (cip_family, soc_major_group, match flags, match_quality, record_id) are deterministic computations that cannot produce null. Physical model declares all 14 columns NOT NULL.

rules.name: has_scorecard_match: expect ~0% TRUE (CIP granularity mismatch)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SUM(CASE WHEN has_scorecard_match THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) <= 5.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk
rules.description: has_scorecard_match should be TRUE for 0-5% of rows. The crosswalk uses 6-digit CIP codes (XX.XXXX) while College Scorecard uses 4-digit (XX.XX), so zero direct matches are expected under strict matching. Threshold allows up to 5% in case the matching strategy changes.
rules.rationale: EDA CRITICAL finding: 'has_scorecard_match will be FALSE for all rows with strict matching' due to CIP granularity mismatch (6-digit crosswalk vs 4-digit Scorecard). Spec acknowledges this (Open Decision #1) and defers to Gold zone. Original spec expectation of 60-90% is incorrect for strict matching. P1 because this is a known design limitation, not a data quality bug.

rules.name: has_bls_match: 90-98% TRUE
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SUM(CASE WHEN has_bls_match THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) BETWEEN 90.0 AND 98.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk
rules.description: has_bls_match should be TRUE for 90-98% of Silver rows. This reflects the strong but imperfect SOC version alignment between the crosswalk (SOC 2018 detailed) and BLS OOH (SOC 2018 rolled-up).
rules.rationale: EDA: 94.6% distinct SOC match rate, but row-level rate is 97.39% because high-frequency SOC codes tend to have BLS matches. Range widened from 90-97% to 90-98% per adversarial audit finding RISK-03.

rules.name: has_onet_match: 88-95% TRUE
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SUM(CASE WHEN has_onet_match THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) BETWEEN 88.0 AND 95.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk
rules.description: has_onet_match should be TRUE for 88-95% of Silver rows. O*NET covers most SOC codes but excludes residual 'All Other' categories.
rules.rationale: EDA: 92.0% of crosswalk SOC codes exist in base.onet_occupations (798 of 867 distinct SOCs). The 69 missing are 'All Other' residual categories that O*NET does not profile. Range 88-95% centered on observed 92.0%.

rules.name: match_quality consistency: all 'no_scorecard' under strict CIP matching
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SUM(CASE WHEN match_quality = 'no_scorecard' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) >= 95.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk
rules.description: Under strict 6-digit CIP matching, match_quality should be 'no_scorecard' for at least 95% of rows (expected 100%). If this drops significantly below 100%, it means has_scorecard_match is returning TRUE unexpectedly.
rules.rationale: EDA Scenario A: With strict 6-digit CIP matching, 100% of 5,903 rows will have match_quality = 'no_scorecard'. Threshold set to 95% to allow for future matching strategy adjustments without immediate failure.

rules.name: cip_family derivation consistency
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE cip_family != LEFT(cipcode, 2)
rules.description: cip_family must always equal the first 2 characters of cipcode. Any mismatch indicates a derivation bug.
rules.rationale: Logical model derivation rule: cip_family = cipcode[:2]. This is a deterministic substring operation. EDA confirms 47 distinct CIP families observed, all consistent with 2-digit prefixes of the CIP codes.

rules.name: soc_major_group derivation consistency
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE soc_major_group != LEFT(soc_code, 2)
rules.description: soc_major_group must always equal the first 2 characters of soc_code. Any mismatch indicates a derivation bug.
rules.rationale: Logical model derivation rule: soc_major_group = soc_code[:2]. EDA confirms 22 valid SOC major groups (after filtering sentinel group 99).

rules.name: match_quality consistent with match flags
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE match_quality != CASE WHEN NOT has_scorecard_match THEN 'no_scorecard' WHEN has_scorecard_match AND has_bls_match AND has_onet_match THEN 'full' WHEN has_scorecard_match AND has_bls_match AND NOT has_onet_match THEN 'partial_no_onet' WHEN has_scorecard_match AND NOT has_bls_match AND has_onet_match THEN 'partial_no_bls' WHEN has_scorecard_match AND NOT has_bls_match AND NOT has_onet_match THEN 'scorecard_only' END
rules.description: match_quality must be correctly derived from the three match flags per the CASE expression in the logical model. Any mismatch means the derivation logic is broken.
rules.rationale: Logical model defines the exhaustive CASE expression. This rule re-implements the derivation as a cross-check to catch any divergence between the transformer's implementation and the model specification.

rules.name: record_id format: xw-<hex>
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE NOT regexp_matches(record_id, '^xw-[a-f0-9]{16}$')
rules.description: Every record_id must match the format xw-<16 hex chars>, computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'xw'. Any format violation indicates a grain computation failure.
rules.rationale: Physical model specifies: 'Deterministic surrogate key: compute_grain_id(row, [cipcode, soc_code], prefix=xw). Format: xw-<16 hex chars>.'

rules.name: BLS SOC referential integrity (informational)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT soc_code) AS unmatched_socs FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM base.bls_ooh b WHERE b.soc_code = base.cip_soc_crosswalk.soc_code)
rules.description: Count of distinct crosswalk SOC codes that do not exist in base.bls_ooh. Expected ~47 due to SOC version granularity differences. Tracked for monitoring, not blocking.
rules.rationale: EDA: 47 crosswalk SOC codes not found in BLS OOH (5.4% of 867 distinct SOCs). All are SOC version granularity differences (detailed vs rolled-up codes). Threshold set to <=60 with margin. P3 because these are known systematic mismatches, not data quality errors.

rules.name: O*NET SOC referential integrity (informational)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT soc_code) AS unmatched_socs FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM base.onet_occupations o WHERE o.bls_soc_code = base.cip_soc_crosswalk.soc_code)
rules.description: Count of distinct crosswalk SOC codes that do not exist in base.onet_occupations. Expected ~69 (residual 'All Other' categories). Tracked for monitoring, not blocking.
rules.rationale: EDA: 69 crosswalk SOC codes not found in O*NET (8.0% of 867 distinct SOCs). These are 'All Other' residual categories that O*NET does not profile individually. Threshold set to <=80. P3 because this is expected O*NET coverage behavior.

rules.name: Scorecard CIP referential integrity (informational)
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT cipcode) AS unmatched_cips FROM base.cip_soc_crosswalk WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM base.college_scorecard cs WHERE cs.cipcode = base.cip_soc_crosswalk.cipcode)
rules.description: Count of crosswalk CIP codes not found in College Scorecard. Expected ~1,949 (all of them) under strict 6-digit matching due to CIP granularity mismatch. This is a known limitation, not a data quality error.
rules.rationale: EDA CRITICAL: Crosswalk uses 6-digit CIP (XX.XXXX), Scorecard uses 4-digit (XX.XX). Zero direct matches exist. All 1,949 distinct CIP codes in the filtered crosswalk are expected to have no Scorecard match. Threshold >= 1900 confirms the expected behavior. P3 informational.
